rationale: The cited paper appears likely substantive rather than incidental because it examines channel relationships with focal constructs that overlap the citing paper’s core variables (interdependence magnitude/asymmetry, supplier commitment/control-related governance). The citing paper explicitly critiques and refines prior interdependence operationalizations, and the cited study is a close prior empirical study in the same domain (supplier–downstream relationships, JMKR 2000). Even though the raw match is only a DOI reference-list hit, this topical and construct-level alignment justifies second-stage review.
